sourceMovie.title: Crawl
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: People revisit Crawl for the same reason it broke through in 2019: clear stakes, memorable faces, and a thriller rhythm that doesn't stall in the middle. Alexandre Aja keeps the ensemble pointed in one direction. The horror and thriller blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: The Mist
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Crawl clicked for you, The Mist (2007) is a strong next stop: both trade in horror and thriller, with unease that lingers after the credits. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Crawl clicked for you, Godzilla (1954) is a strong next stop: both trade in horror and thriller, with unease that lingers after the credits. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

faq.question: What should I watch after Crawl?
faq.answer: Try The Mist, Godzilla, Primate—each shares DNA with Crawl while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is Crawl?
faq.answer: Crawl (2019) is best described as Horror, Thriller. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is Crawl part of a franchise?
faq.answer: Yes—it's part of the "Crawl Collection" storyline. You can enjoy it standalone, but sequels and tie-ins build on characters and continuity established here.
